changelog: Template-updater preserves more customization, and projects can opt out of template skills. (1) agent-merger.js now preserves a tuned `description:` on merge when the agent is personalized (has `nickname`) or specialized (has `## Project Context`). Previously the merger preserved only nickname/aliases, so every update reverted `/review:optimize-agents`- and `/review:specialize`-authored routing descriptions back to the generic template one-liner. Multi-line/folded-scalar safe via new `extractFieldBlock` helper. Thin (un-tuned) agents still take the template description so improvements propagate. (2) Per-project skill opt-out: template-updater.js reads `.claude/update-config.json` (`{"skillExclude": [...]}`) from the TARGET and skips listed skill dirs on every update, so skills a project deliberately removed (e.g. tailwind-css-patterns in a DevExpress/Blazor project, or atomic workflow skills a project consolidated) don't silently return. `update-config.json` is Project zone (added to zone-classifier PROJECT_FILES) — never overwritten. (3) Docs: customization.md zone map + agent update algorithm updated; new `loadExcludedSkills` export. Tests: +14 (description preservation across mergeFrontmatter/mergeAgentFile/extractFieldBlock; skill exclusion across loadExcludedSkills/runUpdate). Targeted suite: 138 passing.
